entry.title: Re: Variable Pay-Requirement-Two different bonus target percentages
entry.content: Thanks for your response. Biggest issue with using ALCF is bringing proration into calculations, i agree with you that business needs to change the process

entry.title: Re: Improved Data Action scoping with the new Data Filter Parameter type
entry.content:
@zee_siddique15 that's going to be possible -> the Data Action can refer to a story filter (also a date range).
But my question @ScottGodfree was more when I want to take a Date Range filter directly from a table or chart widget filter (not from a story filter nor from an input control).
Also my other questions still outstanding: I guess advanced filters are not supported, nor unbooked/master data because this new 'data filter' seems to work on fact data only (I assume), but will try that out for sure once available! 😊

entry.title: Re: SAP PUBLIC CLOUD EVENT - Billing Document Created
entry.content:
Dear @MounaAndre,

I apologize for replying today, but I didn't receive a notification.

To add an automatic header text in a billing document, use SSCUI 101292 Set Up Text Determination for Billing Header.

The configuration is based on three main elements:

  1. Text determination procedure
  2. Text ID assigned to the procedure
  3. Access sequence, which defines where the system copies the text from

Process followed:

1. Open SSCUI 101292

Use the following selection:

Text Object: VBBK
Group for: Billing Header

For a standard customer invoice, select the relevant billing text determination procedure, for example:

T8 Customer Invoice

2. Check the Text IDs in Procedure

Go to:

Text IDs in Procedure

In the tested setup the following entry was used:

Text ID: TX15
Description: Billing Note from Customer
Access Sequence: 501

This means that TX15 is available as a billing document header text and the system can fill it automatically using access sequence 501

3. The sequence in Text IDs in Procedure

The sequence number here only controls the position of the text ID within the procedure.

Example:

Seq. No.: 55
Text ID: TX15
Access Sequence: 501

This sequence does not define the source of the text. The source is defined in the access sequence.

4. Check the access sequence

Go to:

Access Sequences -> Access Sequence for Text IDs

For access sequence 501 the tested entry was:

SeqNo: 10
Text Object: VBBK
Text Object Description: Sales Header texts
Text ID: TX15
All Languages: selected

This means that during billing document creation, the system checks the preceding sales document header for text TX15. If the text exists there, it is copied to the billing document header as TX15

5. The sequence in Access Sequence for Text IDs

The sequence number here defines the search order

Example:

SeqNo 10 = first source checked
SeqNo 20 = second source checked, only if no text is found in step 10
SeqNo 30 = third source checked, only if no text is found in the previous steps

In the tested setup, access sequence 501 has only one step. Therefore the system checks only the sales document header for text TX15

6. Test the setup

To test the process:

  1. Create or open a Sales Order
  2. Maintain header text TX15 Billing Note from Customer

  1. Save the Sales Order
  2. Create the customer invoice
  3. Open the billing document header texts
  4. Confirm that TX15 was copied to the billing document header

7. Important note

Text determination is executed when the billing document is created. Existing billing documents are not updated automatically if the Sales Order text is changed afterwards. The setup applies to newly created billing documents

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

just sharing our experience as we recently went through France 
B2B implementation on SAP DRC Cloud Edition (cf-eu02).

After fixing master data (SIREN/SIRET 14 digits, clean payment 
means, no scheme IDs on IBAN/BIC) the invoice reached the PPF 
with status PA_PROCESS_CREATED and ACK received (ProcessCondition: 
Recevable) 🎉

However, the SAP backend still dumps with ITAB_LINE_NOT_FOUND 
in CL_EDOCUMENT_FR_INV->PROCESS_DRCC_RESP (line 14) because the 
CASE statement on ls_process_instance-status does NOT handle the 
new France B2B statuses (PA_PROCESS_CREATED, PPF_200_*, 
TRANSMIT_CONVERSION_FAILED, etc.).

Has anyone hit the same dump and got a correction note from SAP? 
We have an open incident on CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-FR but would love to 
know if there is already a fix.

Thanks!
Pietro

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 19 - CDS source formatter
Approach to Transfer Inventory for Company that Splits Entities
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

I am currently facing a project, whereby Company A (CoCode 1000, Plant 1000) has split its sub-business process into a new entity that will specialize in that particular business process (Company B with new CoCode 8000, Plant 8000).

However, right now all of Company B's inventories are still in CoCode 1000, Plant 1000 under Company A.

We are in need to move some of Company A's inventory to CoCode 8000, Plant 8000, and its quiet a lot.

Any advice on the strategy for this?

Right now I am proposing the following solution:

- Conduct GR 561 (Initial Stock Balance) in Plant 8000 with the total qty of its inventory.

- Conduct GI 551 (GI Scrapping) in Plant 1000 with the total qty of Company B's inventory as an offset for 561 transaction in Plant 8000.

 

Any advice? Are there any better ways to do this?

Thank you,

Erika

entry.title: Error in Goods Receipt for Purchase Order
entry.content:
I am currently doing Unit 4 Exercise 14 Post Goods Receipt and Invoice Receipt for a Material with Price Control Standard Price on SAP S4500 Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement and I am dumbfounded because previously I posted a Goods Receipt for a Purchase Order for Unit 3 Exercise 12 it went well.

I followed the steps and I don't understand why I am facing this error. What do I do?

entry.title: During FO, when Truck is overloaded, can we still plan FO in SAP TM Basic shipping
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a situation in SAP TM. FO has MOT with 24 Tonne Capacity. But actually it was loaded with 24.5 Tonne.

As per Standard Behaviour , system sets Delivery Block due to Capacity not in line as per MOT.

Can any one please guide how can we remove this block and still plan overload situation? Any BADi which can have tolerance say +1% so even though MOT Capacity is little exceeded, no Block is set.

Thank you in Advance for the Guidance.

BR

entry.title: Error "No authorization to start the UI for transaction" missing catalogs Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently getting error "No authorization to start the UI for transaction" for the following transactions:

We miss the Croatia catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_GL_HR_PC assigned to the following apps and we cannot access (Croatia is not an active country for us):
107024 Assign Payment Type to a Company Code
107025 Assign Payment Method to a Payment Type

We do have these catalogs assigned to the following apps and we still cannot access:

SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_SR_ES_PC
SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_WT_ES_PC
SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_PLN_PC
SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_GL_ES_PC

106351 Define Tax Box Structure Type
106626 Define Withholding Tax Keys Specific to the Country/Region
104311 Assign Country/Rgn Specific Withholding Tax Keys to Withholding Tax Codes
104808 Define Batch Input Session Parameters
106876 Assign Plan Categories to a Virtual Category


Which are the correct catalogs for these apps access?

Note: Provided catalog "SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_AP0_PC Accounts Payable - Configuration" provides access to "Set Up Payment Methods per Company Code for Payment Transactions" (101044), which is a different app.


Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Loan Management - Condition Items
entry.content:

I need to add several condition items in T-CODE:FN1V  with the same condition type and same eff.from.

It comes out an error message:

Condition type Principle part rpymt already available on date 2025.12.18

Message no. 69066

Please let me know how to deal with it.

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community - Mannheim
entry.content: Following the successful route from last year, we will run alongside the Neckar Rive with stunning views and refreshing breeze in the evening. Our meeting point will be at the Alte Feuerwache, in front of the Alte Feuerwache café. From there, we will begin our journey, running alongside the river Neckar and crossing the Neckarschleuse bridge. The trail is approximately 5 kilometers in length.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Workflow has stopped working on some edited PO’s
entry.content:
During a second stoppage to workflow, to make edits to incorrectly transferred PO amounts, workflow has stopped working on the edited PO’s. Has anyone else this situation and how did you fix it?

 

After SAP Fiori migration/cutover, we found some incorrect data, so we turned off workflow to make updates. This was successful within our first month of migration to SAP Fiori. We turned off workflow, stopped users from using the system, made our edits, and turned workflow back on.  This worked perfectly by allowing us to make necessary edits without triggering workflow, and after workflow was turned back on, workflow was working perfectly from that point forward.

 

Last week, after 2 months of review, we found more data issues in another 50 PO’s, so we followed the same process as before. Turned off workflow, stopped users from using the system, made our edits, turned workflow back on, however workflow did not turn on for the edited PO's, and we can’t figure out how to get it working again for those 50 PO's.

entry.title: How to Include/Exclude Time Types into Eligbility for Bonus Calculation
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I know that this topic has been discussed here multiple times, but the suggested solutions do not seem entirely sufficient to me.

We have a customer who requires excluding certain time types from the bonus calculation and including others in the bonus calculation.

Example: Employee had Time Type: Military service – exclude from calculation eligibility

Employee had Time Type: Maternity Protection – include in calculation eligibility

I know that when I include employee status = active in the eligibility rule, the system automatically filters out all time types – meaning the employee is not active during this time.

Which would be great, except for the fact that I need to "ignore" certain time types, i.e., include them in the calculation, such as Maternity Protection, etc.

For reference, the customer has both EC and Time Off. However, I went through Xavier's solution and proposal to include a custom field in Job Info and populate this custom field with the event reason for the given time types, so that the system subsequently adjusts the calculation based on this field:

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/time-off-integration-in-compensation-eligibility-rules/ba-p/13579851

  1. I cannot use a complete exclusion of the employee if they have a certain time type – I need to exclude them only for the duration of this time type.

  2. I want to avoid implementing a custom field in Job Info because the customer has had EC and Time Off for some time now. In this case, it would mean updating every data slice for the given period and event reason that needs to be considered in the eligibility – which would trigger replications to other systems. Furthermore, it would always mean additional maintenance and record updates if a new time type were to be added and included in the calculation.

  3. I want to avoid manual reporting, importing a lookup table, and calculating eligibility based on this lookup table with the number of absence days.

  4. Solution?

Is it therefore possible for this case to implement an MDF object where absence days would be calculated automatically (based on integration) and these would then be included in the worksheet and the respective calculation?

The calculation is well described in this article: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-members/calculate-yearly-absences-pro-ratio-for-compensation-planning/ba-p/14077102

Thank you in advance for your help or for any discussion regarding the implementation proposal.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
Re: Error when using BSUoM on MM to do a outbound task
entry.content:
Hi @Douglass_sepunifei33174 ,

Yes faced such situation like different Sales UOM (Omni Channel DCs). 

Now EWM documentation is good compare to previous.

Copy from EWM documentation:

"

 

  • The outbound delivery item is created in S/4HANA in the base UoM and sent to EWM. It is transferred to the EWM ODO unchanged. The WT is created and confirmed using a stock-specific UoM (SUoM).

  • The outbound delivery is created in S/4HANA in an alternative (not base) UoM and sent to EWM. If this AUoM is maintained as stock-specific in EWM, it is transferred to the EWM ODO as the UoM of the item. If an AUoM is sent from S/4HANA to EWM, it is not used to find a stock which is exactly in this UoM, but the search strategy for picking is relevant. For more information, see Picking.

  • The outbound delivery is created in S/4HANA in an alternative (not base) UoM and sent to EWM. If this UoM is not maintained as stock-specific in EWM, it is transferred to the EWM ODO as the additional quantity role Sales Quantity." Note: Please explore Stock keeping unit of Meaure in EWM, Config in Delivery line item...etc , and see details in below link.

Source: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/109386b7df5842389b52bb2ccb66bae3.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2025.001&q=base%20uom

"An additional point is that we have the new "Batch Specification UoM" flag in EWM, which can be activated or deactivated via SPRO. However, when it is deactivated, it creates queues in the delivery integration with EWM. When it is activated, only deliveries using the basic unit of measure or a single alternative unit can be integrated."

To be honest, never work this Batch Specification UoM in EWM however I got the EWM documentation. I guess quantity roles (check the document below) or stock keeping unit set up missing.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/eba430da2b8b43cea3d455d575db3d55.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2025.001

Please let us know if you have further questions...

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: api_purchaseorder_2 order types FO and GA
entry.content:
Dear community,

I do want to create purchase orders of order type FO and GA by API. I found API_PURCHASEORDER_2 and was able to create common purchase orders with order type NB. But it seems that other order types are not supported according to https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3669206

Anyhow the API always returns HTTP status 201 (created) but no order has actually being created.

As this API seems to be not capable to fulfil my requirement, is anyone aware or an API which allows to create orders of order type FO or GA? Is anyone aware of a Fiori App which can be used for this?

entry.title: Maintenance workflow stuck in approval + Reject not working as expected
entry.content:
we recently introduced a Team Lead approval step by leveraging Flexible workflow for maintenance orders. we really like the concept but there seems a few flunky things happening with this. might be SAP bug, might be our settings. keen to hear views from others:

- 1 WO in approval phase but not showing up in inbox resp person. also not showing up in flex workflow app as "in process" state. so now sort lost in between. any way to give this WO a push

- reject scenario not cleanly going back to workflow initiator. we want to use Reject (+ comment) so we have a daily feedback loop and become better in workprep each & every day. seems not to work as expect yet. hope somebody from SAP product owners read this and thoroughly checks this as good feature to acheive first-time-right-in-full and reduce E2E process friction in all following processes

entry.title: Re: Learning token server deprecation (2027)
entry.content: @EricAbrams I understand where you are coming from. For now the oAuth/saml method is not announced deprecated and when it does, there will be time granted to transition. Therefore, unless you have a business reason to move to OIDC now you don't have to. But you should plan for the upcoming change.

entry.title: Re: Background Job for ME05
entry.content:
Automation Limitations in ME05 Background Processing
Only the three options under *existing records* can be automatically executed in the background. The second screen, which requires the selection and saving of line items, **cannot** be directly automated in the standard ME05 background job.

Try a BDC Wrapper Program
To automate the full process, consider implementing a **wrapper program** that invokes the ME05 transaction in batch mode via a Batch Data Communication (BDC) session. The wrapper program would:

  1. Set initial selection criteria (e.g., purchase requisition numbers, vendors, or dates).
  2. Trigger the BDC session, which simulates the following user actions:
    • Populating the initial ME05 selection screen fields.
    • Navigating to the second screen displaying the source records.
    • Selecting all line items 
    • Save

entry.title: Re: Projecting WBS Element onto Customer Line Items
entry.content:
Dear @VivekDutta,

This is standard system behavior in Public Cloud. There is no standard configuration that copies or derives the WBS Element from the P&L/revenue line item to the Customer/AR reconciliation line item. In the journal entry the WBS Element is stored on the G/L / P&L line item where the project-related revenue or cost is posted. The Customer/AR reconciliation line represents the customer open item from a Financial Operations perspective so the WBS Element remains blank there. Therefore the WBS Element will not be displayed for that customer line item in the Manage Customer Line Items app

More details below:

SAP KBA 3346815 - Profit Center not displayed in Manage Supplier Line Items and Manage Customer Line Items app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP KBA 3465009 - Filtering by WBS Element in Process Receivable apps


Proposal/workaround:

If the requirement is a report showing the customer invoice together with the related WBS Element this can be handled as a reporting workaround instead of changing the standard Manage Customer Line Items app. The clean approach is to build a custom analytical report that combines the customer/receivable line and the relevant G/L/P&L line of the same journal entry where the WBS Element is actually posted. This must be designed carefully, especially when one invoice can contain multiple revenue lines or multiple WBS Elements

More details below:

Report on General Ledger Account Line Items with Project Reference

SAP KBA 3648708 - How to check which G/L account line items have been assigned a WBS element - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

If the business requirement is to have WBS Element directly available in Manage Customer Line Items or other receivables operational apps, this would be a product enhancement request rather than configuration. It should be submitted via SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Product

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects

entry.title: Re: Latest People Profile Cards Configuration - Move to a...

entry.title: Re: Exclude non-working hours in Avg Cycle time in Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content: Hi EWR, A factory calendar table could help you to either manipulate the actual timestamps even (which I do not suggest) or to add columns to the event log for the "corrected" dates. For this example below an event level column could be then adding the vacation day to the c_time for a hypothetical column c_timefactoryincluded. With this, while the process flows would be as expected and as real, for your KPIs and calculations you could then have the "net" cycle times.

entry.title: Innovation Workshop ‘Accelerate Consulting Productivity with SAP Joule 4 Consultants' June 17, 2026
entry.content:
Dear Valued Customer,

The Innovation Workshop focusing on ‘Accelerate Consulting Productivity with SAP Joule for Consultants (J4C)’ took place virtually on June 17, 2026. Please find the agenda for the workshop here.

Please find the recording of the workshop here, the slides shown during the sessions here and the Q&A section here.

The Innovation workshop provided an overview of SAP Joule for Consultants (J4C), highlighting its architecture, UI & Roadmap.

 

 It furthermore provided a live activation of J4C following the main steps from the Access Setup and the validation of the prerequisites to the Configuration Steps and Readiness Validation. The end-to-end setup took appr 1h.

After the setup was completed, the Functional Onboarding steps were outlined. In addition, typical Use Cases and Prompting strategies were demonstrated.

This workshop concluded with key takeaways and further explanation on the commercial model, the availability, the key value proposition and its roadmap and future features. More than 30 customer questions were answered by SAP Product Experts and consultants.

 

Thank you for attending and we hope you benefited from this workshop.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Identity Services Bug in SAP BTP Trial Environment: Current Status
entry.content: @abdul_rahman5  @sasank_mandava-01 Thank you for your question.At this point, we unfortunately cannot provide a temporary workaround that we can confirm would reliably resolve the issue. Our team is continuing to work on the fix, and we will update this post as soon as there are any relevant changes or a confirmed timeline becomes available. We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your patience.

entry.title: Purchase Request – additional expenses (freight) cannot be posted via Service Layer / DI API
entry.content:
Environment

  • SAP Business One 10.0
  • Database: MS SQL Server
  • Service Layer: v2 (OData v4)

Problem

When I create a Purchase Request (oPurchaseRequest) through the Service Layer, the DocumentAdditionalExpenses (freight) collection in my POST payload is silently ignored. The document is created fine, but no row is written to PRQ3 and the document totals do not include the expense. The same collection is also rejected on GET.

Steps to reproduce

  • POST to /b1s/v2/PurchaseRequests with this payload:
 
 
{  "Series": 62,  "ReqType": 12,  "ReqCode": "manager",  "Requester": "manager",  "DocumentLines": [    { "ItemCode": "RM00003", "Quantity": 10, "UnitPrice": 100, "TaxCode": "IGST@28", "WarehouseCode": "REJ" }  ],  "DocumentAdditionalExpenses": [    { "ExpenseCode": 1, "LineTotal": 200, "TaxCode": "IGST@28" }  ]}
  • The document is created (e.g. DocEntry 45). The response contains the line but no DocumentAdditionalExpenses, and DocTotal reflects only line + line tax (1126.40) — the freight is missing.
  • SELECT * FROM PRQ3 WHERE DocEntry = 45 returns no rows.
  • On GET, both options are rejected:
    • ?$expand=DocumentAdditionalExpenses → "Cannot expand invalid navigation property 'DocumentAdditionalExpenses' for entity type 'Document'"
    • ?$select=DocumentAdditionalExpenses → "Property 'DocumentAdditionalExpenses' of 'Document' is invalid"

What I found

The DocumentsAdditionalExpenses child object is documented as applying only to these source tables: INV3, RIN3, DLN3, RDN3, RDR3, QUT3, PCH3, RPC3, PDN3, RPD3, POR3, DRF3. PRQ3 (Purchase Request) is not in that list, which suggests additional expenses aren't supported for Purchase Requests via DI API / Service Layer.

The Document entity is also declared OpenType="true" in $metadata, which I assume is why the unknown collection is accepted but discarded on POST instead of throwing an error.

I can populate freight on a Purchase Request through the B1 client UI (PRQ3 rows do exist for UI-created documents), so the capability exists at the application level but not through the API.

Questions

  • Is the lack of additional-expenses support for Purchase Requests (and Purchase Quotations) in Service Layer / DI API intended and documented?
  • If it is by design, what is the recommended way to capture freight at the Purchase Request stage via the API?
  • Could the API return an explicit validation error when an unsupported collection is sent, rather than silently discarding it?

Thanks in advance.


SAP Business One  OData

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages

entry.title: Re: Material Ledger + Actual Costing rounding difference...
entry.content: Check SAP notes 3090031 and 3292506

entry.title: SAP Layout for Tcode based app is not fixed
entry.content:
Column width is not fixed for transaction based tiles. We can use customizing layout but I am looking for standard view.

Example: Transaction for iDoc Analyzer(ZOTC_IDOCA )
1. In SAP, the layout columns width is OK wrt text in it (Snip attached along)
2. Whereas, in Fiori the column width is tool large than the text result on it (Snip attachedalong). This makes user to scroll a lot on screen to view all results.

entry.title: Cannot create preliminary billing document of Sales order line with item category TAN
entry.content:
The Sales order is not displayed in the app Create Billing Documents. Therefore, it is not possible to create a pro forma invoice before delivering and goods issue of the delivery. 

When item cat CBAB is used, the SO is displayed in create billing documents.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Identity Services Bug in SAP BTP Trial Environment: Current Status
entry.content: @YashikaYTD Thank you for sharing your feedback. We fully understand your frustration, especially as the SAP BTP trial is intended to help new developers and users explore SAP technologies in a smooth and accessible way.Please note that you will not lose the 30 days from your 90-day trial period due to this issue. Once Cloud Identity Services is available again and the issue has been resolved, you can create a new SAP BTP trial account and start with a fresh trial period.Our team is continuing to work on the issue, and we will share updates in this post as soon as more information becomes available.

entry.title: Re: Best practices for connecting Signavio Process Intell...
entry.content:
Hello LeandroRibeiro14 ,

2)
Yes

the local extractor works equally well for S4Hana in Private Cloud environement, e.g. when using I2P  template.
(Verified with the same data scope as an  ECC6 source system, using a migration system  as source)

Additional requirement  is just, that you have the firewall tunneled between the network location of your S4Hana systems and the network location of the local server,  for port 33xx. 

1)
Concerning 'best practice' / good performance:
a)
as this depends higly on the size of your  tables,  it will be be helpful to monitor the  RAM actively used during  extractions,  and based on this possibly modify  the configured maximum-size of the heap-memory used by Java.

My recomendation for achieving an uniterrupted e2e data-flow is , to not let Java use the default G1 garbage-collector in the extractor, but make it use the Shenandoah collector instead. Both is configurable in the start.bat file.

java -Xms8g -Xmx8g ^
-XX:+UseShenandoahGC ^

b)
allign with your SAP Basis concerning all the connection parameters: maximum page-size of the connection might be different vs the template proposal.  Understand how many network threads can run in parallel.

entry.title: The Moving Average Price (MAP) differs for each plant
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,
We are currently facing an issue at GMF where the Moving Average Price (MAP) for the same material master is different across plants.
This discrepancy results in different inventory valuations for each plant and also affects the cost of goods sold (COGS) for the material in each plant.
We would appreciate your assistance in providing a solution. Specifically, we would like to know whether it is possible to maintain a consistent MAP across all plants, or in other words, manage the MAP at the company code level instead of the plant level.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Ruben

entry.title: Cascading Specification Versions Through Inheritance Hierarchies with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
When working with Specification Management, specifications often have parent-child inheritance relationships. When you create a new version of a parent specification, you typically need to create corresponding new versions for all child specifications that inherit from it, and update their inheritance links to point to the new parent version.

This guide walks you through building a SAP Build Process Automation workflow that automates this process. By the end, you will have a fully functional workflow that, given a parent specification version ID, automatically creates new versions of the parent and all its child specifications, and updates the inheritance relationships to maintain the hierarchy with the new versions.


HOW IT WORKS

This workflow automates the cascading of specification versions through inheritance hierarchies. Here's the logic:

  1. Create New Parent Version
    - The workflow receives a parent specification version ID as input
    - It creates a new version of this parent specification
  2. Find All Child Specifications
    - Query the inheritance relationships to find all specifications that inherit from the original parent
    - These are the "children" that need to be updated
  3. For Each Child Specification (Loop)
    - Create a new version of the child specification
    - The new child version initially inherits from the OLD parent version (this is automatic)
    - Find the inheritance relationship record for the new child version
    - Delete the old inheritance link (pointing to old parent)
    - Create a new inheritance link (pointing to new parent)
    - Repeat for the next child
  4. End
    - Once all children have been processed, the workflow ends
    - All new child versions now inherit from the new parent version


PREREQUISITES: IMPORTING THE API ACTIONS

Before building the workflow, you need to import the PLM Specification Management API actions into SAP Build Process Automation.

Step 1: Download the API Specification

  1. Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub (https://api.sap.com)
    2. Search for "Specification Management"
    3. Open the Specification V1 API (https://api.sap.com/api/SpecificationV1/overview)
    4. Click API Specification and download JSON format

Step 2: Upload to SAP Build Process Automation

  1. Open SAP Build and navigate to the Lobby
    2. In the left sidebar, go to Connectors > Actions
    3. Click the Create button
    4. Select Upload API Specification
    5. Upload the downloaded JSON file
    6. Give the Action project a name (e.g., "Specification Management Actions")

Step 3: Add Required Actions

After creating the Action project, you need to add the specific actions you'll use in the workflow:

  1. Open your Action project and go to the Actions tab
    2. Click Add > Available Action
    3. Add the following actions:
MethodAction Description
POSTCreate a new version from an existing specification
POSTCreates a single inheritance relationship between specification versions
DELETEDeletes a single inheritance relationship between specification versions
GETReturns a list of inheritance relationships between specification versions

Step 4: Configure URL Prefix

The URL prefix must be configured for the actions to work correctly:

  1. In the Action project, click the Settings button (gear icon, top right, next to the Release button)
    2. Go to Project Settings > URL Prefix
    3. Set the Resource Path to: /odata/v4/api/specification/v1

This path will be added as a prefix to all action URLs and as a suffix to your destination.

Step 5: Release and Publish

  1. Click Release to create a version of your Action project
    2. Click Publish to make the actions available in your processes

Note: When you add an action to your workflow, the Action project will be automatically added as a dependency to your Process project.


PREREQUISITES: CREATING THE PROCESS PROJECT

Before setting up the workflow, you need to create a Process project in SAP Build:

  1. Navigate to the SAP Build Lobby
    2. Click the Create dropdown button
    3. Select Process Automation

In the Create Project wizard:

Step 1 - Objective:
- Select Automated Process
- Click Next

Step 2 - Type:
- Select Process (Build a business process to automate a workflow)
- Click Next

Step 3 - Name:
- Enter a Name for your project (e.g., "Cascade Specification Versions")
- Optionally add a Description
- Click Review, then Create

After creation, a Create Process dialog will automatically appear:
- Name: Enter a name for the process (e.g., "Cascade Versions")
- Description: Optionally add a description
- Click Create

You will now be taken to the process editor where you can start building your workflow.

Note: Ensure your destination for the PLM Specification Management service is already configured in your SAP BTP subaccount. For more information, see the SAP Help Portal documentation on managing destinations: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/manage-destinations


PREREQUISITES: SETTING UP CUSTOM VARIABLES

Before building the workflow steps, you must define the custom variables in Process Details > Variables > Custom Variables: 

 

Variable Name Type Description
currentChildSpecVersionIdString (T)Stores the current child specification version ID during iteration
parentInheritanceFilterString (T)OData filter for finding inheritance records by source specification
childInheritanceFilterString (T)Combined OData filter for source and target specification
loopIndexNumber (#)Loop counter for iterating through child specifications

To add these variables:
1. Click on the process canvas background (not on any step)
2. Open Process Details panel
3. Go to the Variables tab
4. Expand Custom Variables
5. Add each variable with the appropriate type


STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION

Overview

 

Step 1: Configure the Trigger

The workflow starts with a trigger that receives the specificationVersionId of the parent specification to be versioned.

To configure the trigger input:
1. Click on the Trigger node (start event) in the process canvas
2. In the General tab, open the Inputs section
3. Click Configure next to the Inputs field
4. Add a new input parameter:
- Name: specificationVersionId
- Type: String
5. Click Apply

Input Context:

{
  "startEvent": {
    "specificationVersionId": "<UUID of the parent specification version>"
  }
}


Step 2: Create Parent New Version (Action)

Create a new version of the parent specification.

Action: Create a new version from an existing specification (POST)

Inputs:
id = Specification Version Id from trigger (Process Input)


Step 3: Filter with Source Spec (Script Task)

Build an OData filter to find all inheritance records where the parent specification is the source.

Script:

var specVerId = $.context.startEvent.specificationVersionId;
var filter = "sourceSpecificationVersion_id eq " + specVerId;
$.context.custom.parentInheritanceFilter = filter;


Step 4: Get Parent Inheritance Relationships (Action)

Retrieve all specifications that inherit from the parent specification.

Action: Returns a list of inheritance relationships between specification versions (GET)

Inputs:
$filter = parentInheritanceFilter (Custom Variable)


Step 5: Initialize Loop Variable (Script Task)

Set up a counter to iterate through the child specifications.

Script:

$.context.custom.loopIndex = 0;


Step 6: Loop Condition

Configure a condition to check if there are more child specifications to process.

To add the condition:
1. Click the + button after the previous step
2. Select Controls > Condition
3. Give it a name (e.g., "Loop Condition")

To configure the If branch:
1. Click on the If branch
2. Open the Branch Condition configuration (...)
3. Add the expression:
- First value: Parent inheritance relationships > result > value
- Operator: number of items is greater than
- Second value: loopIndex (Custom Variable)

The Default branch will automatically lead to the End event when there are no more items to process.


Step 7: Get Current ID (Script Task)

Extract the current child specification ID from the array.

Script:

$.context.custom.currentChildSpecVersionId = $.context.action_get_SpecificationVersionInheritanceSources_1.result.value[$.context.custom.loopIndex].specificationVersion_id;

Note: The context variable name (e.g., "action_get_SpecificationVersionInheritanceSources_1") is auto-generated by SAP Build based on the action name and order. To find the correct path in your workflow, use the variable picker on the left side of the script.


Step 8: Create Child New Version (Action)

Create a new version of the current child specification.

Action: Create a new version from an existing specification (POST)

Inputs:
id = currentChildSpecVersionId (Custom Variable)


Step 9: Filter with Source and Target Spec (Script Task)

Build a combined filter to find the specific inheritance record between the new child version and the parent.

Script:

var specId = $.context.action_post_postSpecificationVer__ceV1CopyToNewVersion_2.result.id;
var filter = " and specificationVersion_id eq " + specId;
$.context.custom.childInheritanceFilter = $.context.custom.parentInheritanceFilter + filter;


Step 10: Get Child Inheritance Relationships (Action)

Retrieve the specific inheritance record for the newly created child version.

Action: Returns a list of inheritance relationships between specification versions (GET)

Inputs:
$filter = childInheritanceFilter (Custom Variable)


Step 11: Delete Old Inheritance (Action)

Remove the old inheritance link (which still points to the old parent version).

Action: Deletes a single inheritance relationship between specification versions (DELETE)

Inputs:
id = Child Inheritance Relationships > result > value (Item 1) > id

Note: "Item 1" refers to the first element in the array (index 0). Since our filter is specific, this array should contain exactly one result.


Step 12: Create New Inheritance (Action)

Create a new inheritance link pointing to the new parent version.

Action: Creates a single inheritance relationship between specification versions (POST)

Inputs:
id = (leave empty - auto-generated)
inheritanceTemplate_id = Child Inheritance Relationships > result > value (Item 1) > inheritanceTemplate_id
sourceSpecificationVersion_id = Parent New Version > result > id
specificationVersion_id = Child New Version > result > id


Step 13: Increment Loop Variable (Script Task)

Move to the next child specification.

Script:

$.context.custom.loopIndex += 1;


Step 14: Go to Condition

Loop back to the condition check to process the next child specification, or exit if all children have been processed.

To add the Go to control:
1. Click the + button after the Increment Loop Variable step
2. Select Controls > Go to Step
3. In the configuration panel, select the target step: Loop Condition (from Step 6)

This creates the loop - after processing each child specification, the workflow returns to the condition to check if there are more children to process.

entry.title: 原価センタを無効化する手順と、無効にした際の影響について
entry.content:
原価改定に伴い、原価センタの変更作業を行っております。

つきましては、無効化の手順と無効にした際の影響を確認させてください。

＜手順＞

T-cd :OKEONにて無効化を行う。

＜影響＞

原価改定にて、原価センタを変更した場合の影響を教えてください。

具体的には影響されるカスタマイズやマスタを教えてください。

entry.title: I would like to expose 3 specific views from schema SYS in HANA DB to Datasphere for reporting, how?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm building a "where-used" / impact-analysis report in SAP Datasphere. The idea: for any given connection, list the objects that depend on it (remote tables, views, analytic models), so we can see what would be affected before changing, renaming or removing a connection.

The dependency information I need lives in three HANA system views in the SYS schema:

  • SYS.VIRTUAL_TABLES
  • SYS.OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES
  • SYS.REMOTE_SOURCES

What I'm trying to do: consume these three SYS views inside Data Builder (a Graphical/SQL View) so I can join them and build the reporting view. I only need to read metadata — no business data at all.

What I've already done and observed:

  • Created a SAP HANA connection back to our own tenant database, authenticated with a Database Analysis user.
  • That user can read all three SYS views fine in the Database Explorer (SELECT returns rows).
  • It cannot read any space business data (SELECT on a space table → [258] insufficient privilege) — which is exactly the least-privilege scope I want.
  • But in Data Builder → Import Remote Tables, the SYS schema is not listed (user schemas and DWC_GLOBAL appear, SYS does not), so I can't select these views.
  • Workaround that works: create pass-through views (SELECT * FROM SYS.<view>) in a normal user schema and import those. It works — but I'd rather not create helper views in the database if there's a supported, native way.

My questions:

  1. Is there a supported way to expose/import these three SYS views into Data Builder (via a connection or otherwise) without creating helper views in the database? For example: a way to manually specify a system-schema object during import, a setting to surface system schemas on a connection, or a recommended pattern for consuming SYS catalog metadata in Datasphere.
  2. For a permanent setup, what's the recommended stable, read-only technical user with catalog read access (CATALOG READ) — e.g. a global Database User Group user — given the Database Analysis user is meant to be temporary?

Thanks in advance for any pointers!

entry.title: Re: CFDI Idoc Solution: Idoc not linked with billing docu...
entry.content: Thank you for your quick response and your assessment of the issue. I'm afraid you're right, and that SAP won't provide a solution for this. Nevertheless, I'd like to wait for SAP's response before I develop a custom solution for this.

entry.title: Re: Bypass of standard error message
entry.content:
Dear @Palak_Patel82,

These errors cannot be bypassed during mass PO closure. The Mass Changes to Purchase Orders app is still performing a standard purchase order change/save operation. Therefore, the selected POs must pass the same standard consistency checks as during manual PO change. The app allows mass updates to PO header and item fields but it does not provide an option to skip standard PO validations

More details below:

SAP Help – Mass Changes to Purchase Orders

For MEPO254 Acceptance at Origin cannot be used with AccAsgt categories with no stock the issue is caused by an invalid combination of Acceptance at Origin / Origin Indicator and an account assignment category that is not stock-managed. This needs to be corrected on the affected PO item or in the source/defaulting setup for future documents. It should not be bypassed

More details below:

SAP KBA 3561243 – Error in Creating Purchase Order: Acceptance at Origin cannot be used with AccAsgt categories with no stock

For ME347 Release orders against contract not possible, contract not released, the PO cannot be changed/processed while the referenced contract is not released or while the released status is not correctly reflected in the system. In Public Cloud, SAP documents this scenario for purchase contracts with workflow/status update issues. The contract status must be corrected first, for example by re-triggering the contract workflow/status update as described by SAP and only then the PO closure/change can be processed

More details below:

SAP KBA 3590976 – Error: Release orders against contract not possible, contract not released

Changing these messages from error to warning is also not a reliable standard option in Public Cloud. SAP confirms limitations for maintaining PO processing messages in SSCUI 102364 System Messages for Purchase Order Processing, especially where the message is not available for customer maintenance

More details below:

SAP KBA 2960092 – Convert Messages from Warning to Error or Error to Warning

Therefore the practical approach is to split the affected POs by error, correct the root cause first and then run the mass closure again

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Which SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Apps Are Required for Access Log Management?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am currently working with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and need to create a user who is responsible for access log management.

The purpose of this user is to monitor and review system access information, such as:

  • Who logged into the system
  • When they logged in
  • Login history and access activities
  • Other relevant audit/access log information

I would like to understand:

  1. Which SAP apps should this user have access to?
  2. What is the purpose of each app?
  3. Are there any standard business catalogs or business roles that already include these applications?
  4. Is there a recommended SAP best practice for creating an "Access Log Management" or "Audit Log" user in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Any guidance or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

QuocDT5

entry.title: Re: Approach to Transfer Inventory for Company that Split...
entry.content:
My advice would be avoid 561/551 unless this is a one-off emergency adjustment. For a proper carve-out, set up intercompany STO so that inventory transfer is transparent, auditable, and financially correct.

Below are the comparisons based on which, you can take a call

entry.title: Re: SAP System Refresh - List of Tables to export from Target system as part of preparation activiti
entry.content:
Hello,

stumbled about this blog post... 

You listed ~235 tables to be exported and imported again. However,  it's only a small part of the tables that are to be considered in total. In my small SAP test system (SAP Netweaver AS ABAP 7.52) the PCA tool lists 88 components with 1785 tables. A system with some more installed AddOn's (ECC) lists >120 components with ~2000 tables. So lots of information will be missing after the system refresh, very likely the system will be in an inconsistent shape, showing errors, frustrating users creating tickets. BTW, it does not work completely, for example table RSECTAB will be handled incompletely. 
I don't think it's a good idea doing a system refresh this way. I think doing a system refresh without a dedicated tool will inevitably result in a system refresh of poor quality, causing more costs than those for a dedicated tool.

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: F-90: CREDIT NOTE ISSUE
entry.content: Check SAP note 2514495

entry.title: Re: Head Office & Branch in Customer Master
entry.content:
By default, when a branch customer is linked to a head office, postings (invoices, payments) are consolidated under the head office account. The branch is then used mainly for reporting.

Having said that, the recommendation is

  • If posting must always go to branch, remove the head office assignment in the branch customer master.
  • If sometimes branch, sometimes head office, keep the link but rely on the popup (+) to choose.
  • For reporting only, consider customer hierarchy or grouping instead of head office linkage.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
回复： Link list in capacity scheduling table
entry.content: Hi Jana, does the user have the changing authorizaiton for the orders?

entry.title: Re: Behind the Curtains - what's new about Maintenance Certificates
entry.content:
Hello Dennis,

You have setup two independent federation networks. As per Konstantins answer to 1jules, there is no need to create dedicated federation networks for each use-case.
The general recommendation is to use one Federation Network for both use-cases.

In your case, you correctly determined that adding your SAP Application Server and Hana DB Host Agents to your "SRO" Federation solves the issue.
It does not matter which of the hosts is the Federation Lead. My recommendation would be to choose the one which is most convenient for you.
Let's assume, the Host Agent on SRO is your Federation Lead. You should first reset the Federation on your Hana DB and SAP Application Server and then use the Host Agent on SRO to add them to the existing Federation.

The Reset Federation can be done via the WebUI. Please make sure, you have SAP Host Agent 722 PL70 or higher installed.

If you have further questions, feel free to contact me directly.

Best Regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Recommendations for SAP S/4HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Hi Siva,

Please refer to the SAP Knowledge Article – SAP Learning Curriculum for Professional Services, which consolidates all learning opportunities and guidance:
👉 https://community.sap.com/t5/industries-learning-group-knowledge-base/professional-services-sap-learning-curriculum-for-professional-services/ta-p/14394714 

We are currently working on a hands-on certification specifically for Professional Services, and I will update this Knowledge Article once new information is available.

I recommend following the dedicated Professional Services learning journey for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, which brings together all relevant courses (incl. ISR20, ISR21, ISR22, ISR23, and ISR30), end‑to‑end scenarios, and a practice system where you can do the hands-on exercises:
👉 https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-end-to-end-implementation-operations-sap-cloud-erp-professional-services 

As a prerequisite, start with the baseline certification: 

SAP Certified Application Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Consultant
👉 https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-1 

Stay tuned and follow the Industries Learning Group on SAP Community. 

Thanks,

Greg

entry.title: Saving Variants and Layouts after upgrade
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are currently in process of upgrading our existing S/4 Hana from 2022 to 2025 Version. As end users have created multiple Variants (Public and Private) and layouts in Fiori, Webdynpro and GUI applications in Launchpad.

Could you please let us know, how we can make sure that variants and layouts are not impacted post Upgrade?

Additionally, if possible , can we save these old variants and layouts in the 2025 Version?

entry.title: Re: Error while processing Partial capitalization in KO02
entry.content:
Hi,

As far as I see you are maintaining distribution rule with settlement type PRE - which is for preliminary settlement. This settlement type is to be used in investment scenarios, where certain costs should not be sent as investment costs to asset under construction (AuC). The settlement receiver fur such a type of rule needs to be a "CO object" - which means a cost center, order WBS element,...
An asset cannot be used as receiver for PRE distribution rules.

SAP note 65906 should have some helpful additional details.

Hope this helps,
Kind regards

entry.title: Add fields to existing query
entry.content:
Hello everyone , 

I am new to sap , and i have been assigned the task of adding 2 fields from table MARC to an already existing query.

i looked it up online and apparetly i need to find the proper infoset related to the query but i cant find it .

please if anyone who is familiar with queries can assist me i will be very grateful.

thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: One time Customer pop-up screen
entry.content:
Hi all,

Can I also get some assistance. We have created a foreign cash customer just like we have a local cash customer right? On the local cash customer when creating a sales order, a pop-up screen appears where we can enter further details of the customer but on the Foreign customer we're not getting the pop-up, what could be the issue?

Thanks J

entry.title: How Can I Practice Before Taking the SAP Certified Associate – Security Administrator Exam?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am planning to take the SAP Certified Associate – Security Administrator certification.

Url: SAP Certified - Security Administrator

Before registering for the official exam, I would like to take one or more practice exams to assess my current knowledge and identify areas that need improvement.

I have a few questions:

  1. Are there any official SAP practice exams or mock exams available for this certification?
  2. Does SAP provide any sample questions or exam simulations?
  3. Are there any recommended practice test platforms or question banks that closely resemble the real exam?
  4. Besides SAP Learning, are there any resources (books, courses, GitHub repositories, or community materials) that you would recommend for exam preparation?

I am looking for resources that are as close as possible to the actual exam format and difficulty.

Thank you,

QuocDT5

Any advice or recommendations from those who have already passed this certification would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Manage Customer Line Items – Net Days and WBS Element not displayed
entry.content:
We are reviewing the standard application Manage Customer Line Items and have identified two behaviors that we would like to validate.

1. Net Days field

The customer uses payment terms with a defined number of net due days. However, in the Manage Customer Line Items application, the Net Days field is always displayed as 0, although the payment terms and due date are correctly maintained in the accounting document.

Our understanding is that the application derives this value from the payment terms and baseline date, but in our tests the field is not populated.

Could you please confirm whether this is the standard behavior of the application? If so, under which conditions is the Net Days field populated?

2. WBS Element field

Customer invoices are created with a WBS Element assigned.

The WBS Element is correctly stored in the accounting document and can be displayed in the Display G/L Line Items application.

However, the WBS Element column remains empty

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
Re: Candidate id is not passing from RCM to ONB to EC

entry.title: Re: The Moving Average Price (MAP) differs for each plant
entry.content: MAP is always plant-specific unless you set valuation at company code level. If you are already live, the safer path is to use standard price or Material Ledger transfer pricing rather than trying to force MAP consistency.

entry.title: Movement type 687 hitting BSX transaction key for Both debit and credit side
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on a proof-of-delivery scenario in which the stock-in-transit account needs to be posted during movement type 687. We have maintained the transaction key at the movement type level for stock in transit. However, when posting movement type 687, the system is using transaction key BSX on both the debit and credit sides. It needs to post BSX as a credit and stock in transit as a debit.

Could you please suggest a standard approach to determine the correct G/L account?

 

Thanks,

Sathish

entry.title: Handling “Offers” in Portugal — Configuration and Reporting Guide
entry.content:
In Portugal, offers with a value above €50 are subject to VAT reporting. However, the way this VAT is handled varies depending on the business scenario:

  • VAT charged to the customer: In some cases, the VAT amount is included in what the customer pays.
  • VAT absorbed by the company: More commonly, the company offering the promotion covers the VAT cost itself.

This second scenario introduces additional complexity, as the accounting and tax reporting must remain accurate—even when the customer does not directly bear the VAT.

Recommended Approach for the scenario of "VAT absorbed by the company"

Since there is no specific SAF-T (Standard Audit File for Tax) code designed for these types of documents, companies need a structured workaround to ensure compliance. Below is a recommended approach:

1. Create a Dedicated Tax Code

Start by defining a specific tax code tailored to these offers:

  • Configure the tax code with two account types:
    • Added (positive) and posted to a VAT G/L (account key MWS).
    • Subtracted (negative) and posted to a cost/expense G/L (e.g., a dedicated “offer VAT expense” account, using a second account key such as ESE).

This dual setup allows the VAT amount to be both recognized and offset, reflecting the fact that the company is absorbing the cost rather than passing it on to the customer.

2. Configure Pricing Procedures in SD

To correctly post the VAT impact, you should define two separate SD pricing conditions: 

  • In the first pricing condition assign the VAT account key MWS.
  • In the second pricing condition use an alternative account key, such as ESE.

3. Integrate both Pricing Conditions into Pricing Procedure

Both pricing conditions must be incorporated into the pricing procedure for documents involving offers. This ensures that the correct tax handling is automatically triggered during billing.

4. Posting the Document

When the transaction is posted:

  • The gross value of the document is €0.00.
  • This zero value is the one used for the digital signature.

Despite the presence of VAT calculations in the background, the document itself reflects a net-zero financial transaction from the customer’s perspective.

5. SAF-T Reporting Implications

In the SAF-T file:

  • The tax amount will appear as zero.
  • However, the relevant tax information is still included in the Tax section.

This ensures transparency and compliance with reporting requirements, even when no VAT is effectively collected from the customer.

 

Hope you enjoyed this information.

Cheers,

Elsa

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud - How to post a prior-year direct suppli...
entry.content:
I found the workaround. If you are using the Create Supplier Invoice or Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced app and are unable to post an asset acquisition directly, you can use the following approach:

  1. Create the supplier invoice and Park the document instead of posting it.
  2. A parked document number will be generated.
  3. Open the Manage Supplier Invoices (or the supplier invoice list app).
  4. Search for the parked document using the generated document number.
  5. Open the parked invoice and choose Post.

Using this workaround, the supplier invoice is posted successfully, including the asset acquisition.

I hope this helps anyone facing the same issue.

entry.title: Re: 🇵🇹 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Lisboa, Portugal)
entry.content: great codejam!

entry.title: Re: 🇵🇹 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Lisboa, Portugal)
entry.content: This CodeJam looks highly valuable for anyone exploring enterprise GenAI adoption. Excited to see a hands-on approach covering Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core, LLM deployment, orchestration services, embeddings, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) patterns to improve response quality and reduce hallucinations. Building and querying AI workloads through SAP AI Launchpad and Python SDK while incorporating memory-enabled chatbot capabilities should provide strong practical insights into production-ready AI architectures. Thanks to the organizers and Nagarro for hosting this session!

entry.title: Re: Handling “Offers” in Portugal — Configuration and Reporting Guide
entry.content:
Recommended Approach

To support both VAT treatment scenarios while maintaining compliance and accounting accuracy, the recommended approach is to separate the commercial value of the offer from the VAT settlement logic.

  • Scenario 1 – VAT Charged to the Customer:
    Configure the transaction so the promotional value and applicable VAT are included in the final customer charge. The system should calculate and post VAT as part of the customer-facing invoice amount.

  • Scenario 2 – VAT Absorbed by the Company (Recommended Standard Handling):
    Treat the promotional benefit as the customer-facing value while calculating VAT separately in the background. The VAT amount should be posted to an internal expense or promotional cost account so that:

    • The customer receives the intended offer value without additional charges.

    • VAT obligations remain fully reported and compliant.

    • Financial postings accurately reflect the company-funded tax impact.

Implementation Considerations

  • Introduce a configuration flag to determine whether VAT is customer-paid or company-absorbed.

  • Ensure accounting postings distinguish promotional expense from tax liability.

  • Maintain audit traceability for offers exceeding the €50 reporting threshold.

  • Validate downstream tax reporting and reconciliation processes to avoid discrepancies.

This approach provides flexibility across business scenarios while preserving tax compliance and financial transparency.

entry.title: Productized integrations (Master Data Sync / Hire Sync) when EC is not master
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,
 
I have a question regarding the productized integrations between SAP SuccessFactors (Employee Central / Recruiting) and SmartRecruiters, specifically around Master Data Sync and Hire Sync.
 
In several of my current customer contexts, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central is not positioned as the master system, but rather as a “slave” system, or functional use
 
In this setup, I would like to understand:
 
- Are the standard integrations (Master Data Sync and Hire Sync) fully supported when EC is not the system of record?
- Are there any known limitations or constraints in such architectures?
- What are the recommended patterns or best practices for these integrations when EC is not the master system?
Any feedback or experience on this type of setup would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: The Moving Average Price (MAP) differs for each plant
entry.content: Interesting scenario. Since MAP is typically maintained at plant valuation level, differences across plants can lead to inventory and COGS inconsistencies for the same material. It would be helpful to understand whether there is a standard SAP approach or recommended configuration to align valuation across plants within the same company code while maintaining accurate financial reporting.

entry.title: Re: The Moving Average Price (MAP) differs for each plant
entry.content:
MAP is calculated and consistant either at plant or company level.

In your case, you could consider those options: 

Possible Solutions for Consistent MAP


1. Material Ledger (ML) – Actual Costing (Option Recommended)

Advantages:

Consistent valuation across plants (if configured correctly).
Supports actual costing, providing accurate inventory and COGS calculations.
Compliance with IFRS/GAAP for financial reporting.
Flexibility in costing methods (e.g., standard cost, moving average, actual cost).

Disadvantages:

Higher implementation effort (requires setup of cost components, valuation classes, and period-end closing).
Additional data maintenance (cost estimates, price differences).
Implementation Steps:

Activate Material Ledger (ML) in the company code.
Define valuation strategies (e.g., moving average at company code level).
Configure actual costing (if required for financial reporting).

2. Standard Costing with Periodic Revaluation

Advantages:

Simpler setup compared to Material Ledger.
Consistent standard cost across plants (if maintained at company code level).
Reduced manual effort (no need for daily price updates).

Disadvantages:

Less accurate than actual costing (does not reflect real-time price fluctuations).
Requires periodic revaluation (e.g., monthly/quarterly).
Implementation Steps:

Maintain standard cost at company code level (instead of plant level).
Schedule periodic revaluation (e.g., via MR21 or MR22).

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay-Requirement-Two different bonus target percentages
entry.content: The power of ALCF is the assignments are prorated based on the start and end dates of the assignment. The calculations are done across the assignment and then aggregated on the entry(summary) level

entry.title: Re: Department of Homeland E-Verify Account Certification: What You Need to Know
entry.content: Thanks for sharing @ThomasBilbaugh

entry.title: Re: Portugal Reversal Documents vs. Credit Notes: Key Differences Explained
entry.content:
Hello Marc,

There is no specific process define din Portugal to reverse the downpayment document. You can check the possibility to issue a manual credit memo to the customer. 

Best regards, Elsa

entry.title: Re: Add fields to existing query
entry.content: When displayin query with SQ01, the information is provided:

entry.title: Re: Cannot create preliminary billing document of Sales o...
entry.content: TAN is delivery-related, so it won’t appear in Create Billing Documents until delivery is posted for goods issue. If you need proforma invoices, use billing type F5/F8 or create an order-related item category variant.

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
Hi Prinya,

yes, classic MRP and MRP Live will be used together.  Only MRP Lists and forecast based planning is part of the compatibility scope.

kind regards, Jurgen

entry.title: Regarding Revenue Recognition for Service Orders(revenue recognition key CCOCC)
entry.content:
Hi Expert！

I use Revenue Recognition with the revenue recognition key CCOCC for Service Orders.

I recorded costs and revenue for the Service Order and executed Revenue Recognition as described below,
but the WIP on the G/L account was not cleared.

・Costs：￥1,000
・Revenue：￥0

My understanding is that the Service Confirmation for the Service Order posts ¥1,000 of WIP to the G/L account,
and the execution of Revenue Recognition following the creation of the Billing Document posts a negative ¥1,000 entry for that WIP.

Is my understanding correct?
Under what circumstances would the GL Account WIP be recorded as a negative amount when Revenue are zero?

Soma

entry.title: Need option to post mass Purchase requisitions
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We need a provision to post mass Purchase Requisitions.

Currently, we are creating more than 50 PRs daily, and each PR contains at least 100 line items. Due to the high volume, manual creation is time-consuming and inefficient.

We request a provision for a mass PR upload template through the migration object or any other suitable method to facilitate bulk creation of Purchase Requisitions.

Kindly review and provide the possible options.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Planned Delivery Cost of Purchasing in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @Wei_Wum8,

Thanks for the feedback and great question — this is a known gap in the migration template.

Root cause: In SIF_PURCH_INF_V2, the conditions sub-sheet does not expose a separate column for the freight supplier
on FVA1 lines. All condition rows inherit the info record's main supplier, so there is currently no way to assign a
different freight vendor during the migration run itself.

Post-migration workaround via OData API: After migration, you can mass-update the freight supplier using the standard
OData API API_PURGPRCGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV. The field you need is Supplier on the entity A_PurgPrcgCndnRecdValidity —
this is exactly the "Supplier" field visible on the Detail → Control tab in the Fiori app. You can PATCH it per
condition record to set the correct freight vendor.

Steps:
1. Query A_PurgPrcgCndnRecdValidity filtered by ConditionType eq 'FVA1' to retrieve all migrated freight condition
records
2. For each record, PATCH the Supplier field with the correct freight vendor account number
3. The key fields to identify each record are ConditionRecord + ConditionValidityEndDate

This is not ideal as a two-step process, but it is a fully supported, scriptable path using the released API. I would
also recommend raising a product feedback request to SAP to have this column added to the SIF_PURCH_INF_V2 template in
a future release.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: HTTP 403 Forbidden when SAP CPI calls On-Premise HTTP...
entry.content: @Deepak161 if you provide the port number :443 at the end it may work. We have cases where we use HTTPS through cloud connector, but the one thing we cannot do is put https:// at the beginning of the URL in CPI.

entry.title: Re: SAC and BTP subaccount Provisioning
entry.content:
Hi

You can provision your users from IAS to various target ssystem as described in https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/target-systems

You'll see a target system to provision SAC and for BTP you should use SAP BTP XS Advanced UAA (Cloud Foundry).

The provisioning to these systems might be complex to configure via IPS depending on your scenario. I'd rather configure authentication to both system as recommended via Cloud Identiy Services and use the "groups" attribute from OIDC token to pass the permissions as part of the authentication process. These groups can be easily mapped to role collections in BTP subaccount and Teams for example in SAC. No IPS is needed in this scenario and users are automatically created if you enable shadow user creation

If you still want to use IPS you'll see that provisioning is possible to various types of systems, but for example, using Entra as Target system requieres IAG license.

Regarding your second question, you cannot connect GRC to IPS at the moment to provision the access. I've seen that new version of GRC comes with a connector for Cloud Identity Services but as far as i could check it only serves as Datasource but they haven't enabled provisioning from GRC to Cloud Identity Services

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 13 - Optional CDL keyword
